# Fixture And Oracle Provenance

The hand-written fixtures under `tests/fixtures/` are small format examples for
unit tests. External compatibility proof is generated by
`tests/oracle/pennmush_oracle.py` instead of storing mutable PennMUSH dump files
in the repository.

## Compatibility Matrix

| Family | Repo fixture | Source authority | External acceptance |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| current main | `main/current_objects.db` | `src/db.c`, `dbtools/db_labelsv1.cpp` | PennMUSH server load oracle uses a server-dumped minimal main DB and reloads canonical writer output. |
| oldstyle main | `oldstyle/new_strings_no_labels.db` | `dbtools/db_oldstyle.cpp` | Current upgrade output is accepted by the current reader and by the server load oracle when used as the main DB shape. No bundled historic dump is stored; unsupported numeric bit conversions are explicit parse errors until a source fixture requires them. |
| mail | `mail/current_with_alias_message.db` | `src/extmail.c`, `src/malias.c`, `hdrs/extmail.h` | PennMUSH server load oracle accepts writer output for maildb in uncompressed, gzip, and bzip2 runs. |
| current chat | `chat/current_channel.db` | `src/extchat.c`, `hdrs/extchat.h` | PennMUSH server load oracle accepts writer output for chatdb in uncompressed, gzip, and bzip2 runs. |
| oldstyle chat | `chat/oldstyle_channel.db` | `src/extchat.c` oldstyle fallback path | Oldstyle fixture upgrades to canonical current chat output, which is covered by the server load oracle. |
| compression | generated in tests | `src/game.c`, `dbtools/io_primitives.cpp` | Server oracle covers uncompressed, gzip, and bzip2 main/mail/chat sets; explicit external-filter support is covered by local copy/fail filter fixtures. |

The oracle uses the checked-out PennMUSH tree at:

```text
C:\Users\Q\src\pennmush
```

It verifies the server with:

```powershell
wsl -u q -- bash -lc "cd /mnt/c/Users/Q/src/pennmush && ./src/netmud --version"
```

The expected server reports:

```text
PennMUSH 1.8.8 patchlevel 0
```

Each oracle run creates a fresh WSL game directory under:

```text
/tmp/mushy-peas-penn-oracle
```

The harness copies `game/`, creates `mush.cnf`, `alias.cnf`, `restrict.cnf`,
and `access.cnf`, patches only the copied config, and runs:

```text
timeout -s INT 6s /mnt/c/Users/Q/src/pennmush/src/netmud --no-session mush.cnf
```

Baseline main-database provenance:

- PennMUSH creates a minimal world when `data/indb` is absent.
- The oracle reads the server-dumped `data/outdb*`.
- `mushy_peas` parses that real main DB and writes canonical current output.

Mail and chat provenance:

- Mail and chat baseline files are generated through the package writers.
- PennMUSH then loads main, mail, and chat together in the server oracle.
- The oracle checks explicit load markers for all three database files.

Compression cases:

- uncompressed: `data/indb`, `data/maildb`, `data/chatdb`
- gzip: `data/indb.gz`, `data/maildb.gz`, `data/chatdb.gz`
- bzip2: `data/indb.bz2`, `data/maildb.bz2`, `data/chatdb.bz2`

The oracle rejects logs containing mail/chat load errors even if startup reaches
`RESTART FINISHED.`.
